metadata.name: Global Tariff Briefing
metadata.audience: NYT colleagues tracking tariffs and trade policy globally

all.headline: Oil Rally Builds as ‘Staggering’ Middle East War Jolts Energy
all.description: Oil hit $85 a barrel for the first time since July 2024 as the war in the Middle East escalates.

all.headline: European Stocks Tumble as Inflation and Rate Outlook Darkens
all.description: European stocks headed for the biggest two-day drop since April as conflict in the Middle East intensified, casting doubt over the inflation and monetary policy outlook.

all.headline: Emerging Assets Extend Slide as Inflation Risks Get Priced In
all.description: Emerging-market assets extended losses as the war in Iran impedes a key route for global energy supplies, lifts the dollar and forces investors to adjust to the risks of resurgent inflation.

all.headline: European Gas Hits Three-Year High as Qatar LNG Halt Rocks Market
all.description: European natural gas prices surged more than 40% to the highest since 2023 amid uncertainty over how long the shutdown at the world’s largest LNG export plant in Qatar will weigh on global energy supp

all.headline: Bonds Slump as Inflation Risk Mounts From War in Iran
all.description: Oil price surge is prompting traders to scale back bets on easier monetary policy.

all.headline: Gold Dips as Stronger Dollar Offsets Middle East Risk Premium
all.description: Gold fell after a four-day rally, as traders weighed the escalating war in the Middle East against the prospect of a stronger dollar and elevated inflation.

all.headline: Young: Prolonged War Will Lead to High Gas Prices
all.description: The Iran war has put energy assets at risk in the Middle East. Limited oil price surges signal that traders are betting on a short conflict but a lengthy disruption threatens to cause chaos across mar

all.headline: BlackRock Says Index Funds Alone Aren’t Enough for US Retirees
all.description: BlackRock Inc. called for more active management and the inclusion of private assets in 401(k)s, contending index investing alone is no longer sufficient to manage risk and fund retirements that can n

all.headline: SpaceX $1.75 Trillion Valuation Is ‘Justifiable,’ PitchBook Says
all.description: A $1.75 trillion valuation for billionaire Elon Musk’s SpaceX in an initial public offering would be “justifiable,” according to a PitchBook analyst.

all.headline: Svelland's Wiggen: Market Not Prepared For GCC Attacks
all.description: Energy markets weren't prepared for attacks on GCC countries by Iran, as part of the retaliation against strikes from the US and Israel, says Svelland Capital Director Nadia Martin Wiggen. Discussing

all.headline: Gas and oil prices soar and shares tumble as crucial shipping lane threatened
all.description: Iranian official threatens to "set fire" to any ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz.

all.headline: 'I've given up on working in hospitality. The £15,000 pay isn't worth the stress'
all.description: People in their 20s and 30s tell the BBC how they feel about their finances ahead of the chancellor's Spring Statement.

all.headline: Amazon says drones damaged three facilities in UAE and Bahrain
all.description: The incidents highlight the vulnerability of key technology infrastructure during military conflicts.

all.headline: What to expect from the Spring Statement
all.description: Chancellor Rachel Reeves will give an update on her plans for the UK economy when she gives a statement alongside an economic forecast on 3 March.

all.headline: Know when to fold them: the tech inspired by origami
all.description: Origami techniques can add strength to structures without adding bulk.

all.headline: Will petrol and diesel prices go up now?
all.description: If oil prices remain high for some time, the knock-on effects could affect the costs of fuel and food

all.headline: Bars close and hundreds lose jobs as US firm buys Brewdog in £33m deal
all.description: Beverage and cannabis company Tilray acquires the brewery, the brand and 11 bars after Brewdog went into administration.

all.headline: Limited flights leave UAE but disruption continues amid Iran strikes
all.description: The majority of flights to key Middle Eastern hubs have been cancelled for Monday as the conflict continues.

all.headline: What are my rights if my flight is cancelled or delayed?
all.description: We look at the different circumstances that affect you if you're due a refund for cancelled or delayed flights.

all.headline: UK launches consultation asking for views on under-16s social media ban
all.description: Discussions over what measures to implement to protect children's wellbeing will last for three months.

all.headline: Six US soldiers killed in Iranian strike on Kuwait base
all.description: The US defence secretary confirms a US military base was hit by an Iranian missile on Sunday.

all.headline: 'They hit so hard the house was shaking': Iranians describe impact of US-Israel attacks
all.description: People in Iran tell the BBC they hear explosions day and night, as the US and Israel continue striking the country for a third day.

all.headline: France to boost nuclear arsenal and extend deterrence to European allies
all.description: Emmanuel Macron said eight countries could enjoy protection from France's nuclear umbrella - but that Paris would retain sole decision-making power.

all.headline: At least 169 people killed in South Sudan 'surprise' attack
US sanctions Rwanda's military and top commanders over fighting in DR Congo
all.description: Rwanda accused Washington's move as “one sided” and politically motivated.

all.headline: India and Canada reset ties with 'landmark' nuclear energy deal
all.description: A host of deals, including one to supply India with uranium, is unveiled as Mark Carney meets Narendra Modi in Delhi.

all.headline: Lost Rembrandt painting rediscovered after 65 years
all.description: The painting, created by the Dutch master in 1633, had disappeared after being sold  in 1961.

all.headline: Saturday Night Live criticised for 'hurtful' Tourette's sketch
all.description: The condition is "not a joke", the Tourette Action charity says, as the Baftas fallout continues.

all.headline: Texas authorities identify Austin bar shooting victims
all.description: Three victims and the suspected gunman were killed, and more were injured in the Sunday shooting that the FBI says could have terrorism links.

all.description: Nicosia wants European partners to step up in defense of the Mediterranean island, as Iranian drones menace the region.

all.headline: Trump says UK-US relationship ‘not like it used to be’
all.description: The U.S. president said he now has "very strong relationships" with other countries in Europe.

all.description: It’s spring forecast day, but as the war continues across the Middle East is the special relationship now under threat? Sir Keir Starmer and Donald Trump have traded blows on the legality of the

all.headline: Public sector AI: Shifting from ambition to readiness
all.description: In closing this gap, Europe can realize smarter, more trusted public services for every citizen while demonstrating global leadership.

all.headline: Iran is not Venezuela as much as Trump wants it to be
all.description: With no clear successor and much of Iran’s power structure still intact, the White House faces a far more complex fight than it did in Venezuela.

all.headline: Caught between wars
all.description: Presented by Amazon

all.headline: Wie der Iran-Einsatz zum unberechenbaren Risiko wird
all.description: Während Donald Trump Bodentruppen im Iran nicht mehr ausschließt und die Angriffe bis in den April dauern könnten, ist Friedrich Merz in Washington angekommen. Es ist eine Reise unter maximalem Druck:

all.headline: Q&A: Families shouldn’t have to coordinate Sweden���s rare disease care
all.description: A Q&A with Zozan Sewger Kvist, CEO of rare disease competence center Ågrenska, on stalled reform, uneven access to care in Europe and the limits of regulation.

all.headline: Investing in cancer innovation
all.description: Funding oncology research is not just a moral imperative, but also a strategic choice that can drive competitiveness and productivity

all.headline: Why Khamenei’s killing hit Putin where it hurts
all.description: U.S. strikes on Iran will have deepened the Russian president’s paranoia, and his desire to win in Ukraine.
